articles.title: Trump tells allies 'go get your own oil' from Strait of Hormuz

children.content: The US president again attacks America's allies over their reluctance to get involved in the Iran war, warning them the "US won't be there to help you anymore, just like you weren't there for us".

image.altText: Donald Trump wearing a blue suit and tie gesturing with his right hand while standing at a US presidential lectern

articles.title: Trump to welcome King for ‘terrific’ state visit amid boycott calls over Iran war

children.content: US President Donald Trump lashed out at the UK just before the visit was confirmed as France, Spain and Italy restrict airspace to US war planes.

image.altText: Donald Trump and King Charles in black tuxedos sitting next to each other at a table with flowers

articles.title: Air travel in the Middle East may be disrupted but Lebanon is unfazed

children.content: Beirut-Rafic Hariri Airport, Lebanon's gateway to the world, is still operating even as Israel bombards its surroundings.

image.altText: An airport hall with lined floor, seats, a glass ceiling, and no people around

children.content: US President Donald Trump says the US will leave Iran "very soon" has given an estimate of "two weeks, maybe three". Follow live.

items.title: Trump says US will leave Iran 'very soon' and estimates 'two weeks, maybe three'

updates.title: ATO agrees to temporary relief for businesses, Chalmers says
Trump confirms the US is 'finishing the job' in Iran
US to leave Iran within two to three weeks, Trump says

items.title: Iran sets 24-hour deadline on missile threat. Here's what you should know this morning

image.altText: A man wearing a helmet and flak jacket with the word PRESS on it looks at the camera with building rubble behind him

children.content: US companies including Microsoft, Google and Apple could be targeted, while Donald Trump urges the world to buy American fuel.

items.title: US journalist kidnapped in Baghdad

children.content: Police in Iraq say they are searching for an American journalist who has been kidnapped in Baghdad, possibly by an Iranian-backed militia group.

image.altText: Shelly Kittleson stands in front of a sunset and the site of ruins.

items.title: Mine may be shut down amid money laundering allegations, bikie claims

children.content: The Tasmanian government has taken steps to revoke the operating licence of one of the state's biggest mining operations, after being warned it was allegedly used as a "vehicle for money laundering" and former owners were associated with underworld figures.

image.altText: An aerial of mining buildings in a forest and huge mountains in the background.

items.title: Public should not expect clear trigger for fuel rationing

children.content: The federal government is not expected to publicly nominate a clear trigger point for fuel rationing, despite internal modelling outlining potential thresholds.

articles.title: A recession could happen even without a fuel shortage. Here's how

children.content: The government is urging people to carry on as normal, despite fuel shortages, because it knows that panic will make matters worse. But it may need to consider more drastic economic intervention.

image.altText: Two men in suits address the media.

articles.title: Motorists may not see cheaper fuel for days or weeks

children.content: Labor says it may take a couple of days, or even weeks if for those living in regions where fuel stocks are not turned over quickly, for the savings from the fuel excise cut to flow through. Follow live.

image.altText: Two fuel nozzles with signs saying :( SORRY HOSE NOT IN USE

thumbnail.altText: A person paying for fuel at petrol station

items.title: One of Australia's biggest 'giveaway' clubs has its licence cancelled

children.content: RS Rewards claims to have given away more than $10 million worth of cars, houses and cash. But its licence has just been cancelled.

image.altText: A luxurious house on the gold coast with a large pool, surrounded by palm trees.

items.title: Childhood vaccine coverage declines for fifth year in a row

children.content: The latest immunisation data shows more than 80,000 Australian children aged five and under are not fully up to date with their vaccinations.

image.altText: A nurse holds a needle up to a young boy's arm

items.title: Dezi Freeman died isolated from the modern world, but not far from it

children.content: The fugitive resurfaced more than 150 kilometres away from the town where he shot and killed two police officers seven months ago. Mystified locals are questioning how.

articles.title: 'Evil inside him': Dezi Freeman's nephew speaks out on cop killer uncle's death

children.content: When Dezi Freeman was shot dead, it left his family with mixed emotions. Some are shattered, others know an "evil man" is dead. His nephew speaks out.

image.altText: Two men smiling for a photo in a backyard.

articles.title: Freeman joins a long line of fugitives captured or killed in Australia

children.content: Australia has a long history of violent outlaws heading bush to avoid capture. But how is it still possible to remain on the run for so long?

image.altText: A photo of Dezi and an image of Ned Kelly

gallery.altText: A road leading through Thologolong, with a blue and white sign beside the tree-lined road.

items.title: ASX jumps on back of massive Wall St rally amid war end hopes

children.content: Wall Street ends a weak month with a massive gain, as investors eye a possible end to US military action in Iran, and Australian shares follow. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: A close up of Donald Trump.

items.title: Anger at developer's plan to build $10m mansion on illegally cleared land

children.content: A property developer who illegally cut down hundreds of native trees has lodged a new plan to build a lavish $10 million, six-bedroom mansion in Sydney's south.

image.altText: A render of a house.

items.title: House prices rise everywhere besides Sydney and Melbourne

children.content: There is a significant divide between property price growth in major cities as buyers weigh up interest rate hikes and geopolitical tensions.

gallery.altText: A man and a woman smile at the camera.

An auction agent trying to sell a home in front of a crowd of three dozen people, outside an apartment complex.

items.title: Less than half of states signed up as national gun reform deadline passes

children.content: The federal government's March deadline for national gun reform has come and gone, with more than half the states and territories still opposed to parts of the plan. Only four have committed to a gun buyback scheme.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese wearing glasses and looking stern.

items.title: Socceroos headliners shine, but will the experimentation pay off at World Cup?

children.content: The Socceroos are done with experimenting, but now need to find the winning formula with the World Cup quickly approaching.

image.altText: Socceroos player Nestory Irankunda holds his crotch with one hand and points other hand with silver glove

items.title: This controversial rom-com starring Zendaya is one of the best movies of the year

children.content: On the surface, A-listers Zendaya and Robert Pattinson's new film seems like a regular rom-com. That's exactly what director Kristoffer Borgli wants you to think.

gallery.altText: A man dips a woman while they smile at each other

items.title: From Brisbane to the world: Joff Bush on the music behind Bluey

children.content: A new orchestral Bluey album is out. Lead composer Joff Bush talks about classical music, storytelling and inspiring kids to love music.

image.altText: Bluey composer Joff Bush seated in his production studio, surrounded by screens and speakers.

items.title: 'Nothing's out here' Vodafone ad angers rural Australians

children.content: A new Vodafone campaign has regional Australians seething after the telecommunications giant claimed to focus its network infrastructure on where "98 whatever per cent of you actually live".

items.title: Could I be using E10 in my car?

children.content: Australia's leading motoring organisation is urging drivers to fill up with E10 fuel in an effort to conserve national fuel supplies. But what exactly is E10? And could it help ease the current fuel crisis?

image.altText: A man holds a petrol bowser while filling up his car.

items.title: Fuel prices drive up already costly venture of navigating drought

children.content: Whether feeding or selling, the cost of diesel is becoming an added headache for drought-hit farmers in NSW.

image.altText: A ute drives along a road where black cattle graze either side.

items.title: Hundreds of thousands in flood damages leave tourism vendors devastated

children.content: After multiple major weather events, tourism vendors are looking at clean-up costs in the hundreds of thousands, with some businesses lost entirely. The industry is calling for governments to step in and help.

image.altText: A kitchen full of floodwater.

items.title: Vision loss and self-harm — government warned of custody health risks

children.content: Internal government memos obtained by the ABC detail a series of health incidents in Northern Territory watch houses and a suicide attempt in the Holtze prison.

image.altText: Glass jail walls and a red duress button

items.title: Jewish people scared to join Bondi inquiry, former royal commissioner says

children.content: Ronald Sackville who led the disability royal commission says it is unlikely the the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion can properly examine the experiences of Jewish Australians before its December 2026 deadline.

image.altText: Floral tributes on promenade at Bondi Beach

items.title: Tiger Woods had opioid pills in pocket at crash scene, police say

children.content: A sheriff's office report says golfer Tiger Woods had hydrocodone opioid pills in his pocket when interviewed at the scene of his car crash in Florida last week.

image.altText: Golfer Tiger Woods stands by his overturned vehicle

items.title: Judge halts Trump's White House demolition and ballroom project

children.content: A federal court has ordered the Trump administration to suspend work on a $400 million ballroom on the site of the demolished East Wing of the White House, barring construction from proceeding without congressional approval.

items.title: The list that helps Aussie surfing star handle the noise in her head

children.content: At just 17, Emerson Jones is a chance to make her Billie Jean King Cup debut after being named in Australia's squad to face Great Britain.

items.title: Shein and Kmart accused of copying Queensland retailer's designs

children.content: Queensland business Sabo Skirt has claimed Shein, Kmart and numerous other retailers and wholesalers have copied its clothing patterns, prints and trademarks on "lower quality" garments in a federal court claim.

items.title: Cocoa prices crash but Easter chocolate stays expensive

children.content: Consumers cannot expect to find cheaper chocolate on supermarket shelves this Easter despite a major drop in the world cocoa price, industry experts say.

image.altText: Close up of small easter eggs in a bowl.

items.title: Why your household could be hit by a bill hike trifecta this month
